commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=707ef6485971cde45bc2d62e2d75c3035fb513a6)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an `OPENCLAW_ENABLE_GOOGLE_GEMINI_CLI_HARNESS` env gate for Google Gemini CLI setup/runtime registration, updates Google setup copy, adds focused tests, and adds a Google provider rebuild note.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +97, Docs +45. Total +160 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main resolves `google-gemini-cli` through default setup/runtime registration, while this patch makes those registrations absent unless a new env flag is set. I did not run a live onboarding or upgrade flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed to choose whether `google-gemini-cli` can become opt-in-only; automation cannot supply the missing compatibility/product decision or the contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: feat(google): gate Gemini CLI harness behind explicit opt-in
target.body:
## Summary

Continues the Google provider rebuild by moving the deprecated Gemini CLI harness out of default Google registration paths.

- adds an explicit `OPENCLAW_ENABLE_GOOGLE_GEMINI_CLI_HARNESS` opt-in policy
- keeps the bundled Google plugin on the official `google` provider path by default
- registers `google-gemini-cli` provider and backend only when the opt-in flag is enabled
- stops the lightweight Google setup entry from registering the Gemini CLI backend unless that flag is enabled
- updates Google API key wizard copy so it no longer describes the default Google group as `API key + OAuth`
- adds regression tests for default-disabled and explicit-enabled behavior in both setup and bundled plugin registration
- documents the intended Google provider rebuild boundary and follow-up work

## Why

The current Google surface mixes official API-backed providers with a CLI-backed harness path. The Gemini CLI OAuth/project discovery path can depend on Cloud Code Assist internals such as `cloudcode-pa.googleapis.com`, `v1internal:loadCodeAssist`, and `v1internal:onboardUser`. Those should not be required by default Google setup or default plugin registration.

This PR does not delete the Gemini CLI harness. It keeps it available for deliberate opt-in while making the supported default Google path API-backed and explicit.

## Follow-up scope

- Remove Gemini CLI OAuth from default onboarding metadata or mark it manual-only behind the same opt-in boundary.
- Add a first-class Gemini API provider implementation for the current official API surface.
- Keep Vertex AI separate from AI Studio / Gemini API key auth.
- Remove Cloud Code Assist project discovery from provider auth paths.
- Add regression tests proving normal Google setup never touches Cloud Code Assist internal endpoints.

## Test plan

Not run locally from this connector session.

Recommended targeted check:

```bash
pnpm vitest run extensions/google/gemini-cli-harness-policy.test.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts extensions/google/index-harness-registration.test.ts
```
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
